plan.description:
This DMP is part of a project that will be happening at the Institute of Marine Research (IMR) that is focused on the effect of ocean acidification on Ammodytes marinus embryos. The data that will be collected are daily hatch frequencies, hatching success, time to hatch, and body size metrics at hatch. This will be in addition to metadata which will include the treatments, tank numbers and other important identifier details.
The purpose of this DMP will be to ensure that the data created in this experiment is stored properly and shared with the public as needed.
plan.title: A. marinus OA Embryos DMP

blueprint.label: AQUASERV DMP Blueprint

systemField.label: Title of Plan
systemField.description: Please provide a name for your DMP Plan

systemField.label: Description
systemField.description: Briefly describe the context and purpose of the DMP

systemField.label: Language
systemField.description: Select the language of your DMP

referenceField.label: Researchers
referenceField.description: Add here the names of people that have produced, processed, analysed the data described in the DMP.

referenceField.label: Organizations
referenceField.description: Add here the names of the organizations contributing to the creation and revision of the DMPs

extraField.label: Introduction
extraField.description: Ethical considerations in science become crucial whenever your research involves potential harm to living organisms, ecosystems, or communities. If your research fits into these criteria please answer following questions. As a default please fill the Horizon Europe - Ethics template, however other templates are available.

extraField.label: Introduction
extraField.description: Please select a template to describe your dataset that best fits your research topic. You can chose more than one template. All the templates prepared for the AQUASERV project are called "AQUASERV | category | datatype". If you can't find a template that fits your research, please contact aquaservhelpdesk@ualg.pt

blueprint.description: This is the main AQUASERV blueprint containing common questions about you and your project, as well as multiple "templates." The common questions need to be answered only once. Use one "template" for each dataset you are generating. For example, if you generate genomics data, use the genomics template (already available); if you generate LC-MS data, use the LC-MS template (coming soon). If you generate more than one type of dataset, you can combine multiple templates into one DMP "plan."

blueprintValue.value: When looking at ethical principles, ideally alternatives can be used to animals in the experiments. In the case of our study, this is not possible. One of the major goals of this study is to compare its results to those of previous studies. As a result, we need to duplicate the procedures used to the best of our abilities. Thus, we will need to use live adults, embryos, and larvae as apart of this experiment. We have considered alternatives to these experiments, but there are none. Secondarily, we plan to use the number of animals needed to achieve statistically useful results, but no more than the necessary. In addition, fish embryos are considered alternative animal use as they are considered in vitro for experimental purposes. The methodologies being used will impact the welfare of the animals, but impacts are attempting to be minimized in a few ways. Transport stress of adult sandeels will be minimized through frequent water changes and sand included in their transit tank, allowing for natural burying behavior. Embryos will be exposed to suboptimal water quality. The manipulation of temperature and CO2 is an inherent and unavoidable part of our research. Finally, some individuals will need to be humanely euthanized via an overdose of MS-222 for preservation and later analysis. Finally, the Austevoll Research Station, Institute of Marine Research, Storebø, Norway, is a certified research facility for fish atall developmental stages and holds permits from the Norwegian Directorate of Fisheries to catch and maintain sandeel. All experimental protocols and procedures will be conducted in accordance with approved guidelines submitted to the Norwegian Food Safety Authority’s animal research unit in Maatilsynet under the current KnowSandeel project application(FOTS ID 31098) - FOTS=“Forsøksdyrforvatningen tilsyns- og søknadssystem” – 48 months authorization. These permits and protocols comply with ethical regulations and legislation.

This DMP is part of a project that will be happening at the Institute of Marine Research (IMR) that is focused on the effect of ocean acidification on Ammodytes marinus embryos. The data that will be collected are daily hatch frequencies, hatching success, time to hatch, and body size metrics at hatch. This will be in addition to metadata which will include the treatments, tank numbers and other important identifier details.
The purpose of this DMP will be to ensure that the data created in this experiment is stored properly and shared with the public as needed.

description.description: This description is being used to ensure that the ethics of sharing data are considered, if any of the data we will be creating is considered sensitive.
description.label: Horizon Europe - Ethics

descriptionTemplate.label: Horizon Europe - Ethics
descriptionTemplate.description: A template of Horizon Europe about ethics.

fieldSet.title: Are there, or could there be, any ethics or legal issues that can have an impact on data sharing? These can also be discussed in the context of the ethics review. If relevant, include references to ethics deliverables and ethics chapter in the Description of the Action (DoA).
Will informed consent for data sharing and long term preservation be included in questionnaires dealing with personal data?

field.textValue: We are not dealing with personal data of people.

description.description:
This DMP is part of a project that will be happening at the Institute of Marine Research (IMR) that is focused on the effect of ocean acidification on Ammodytes marinus embryos. The data that will be collected are daily hatch frequencies, hatching success, time to hatch, and body size metrics at hatch. This will be in addition to metadata which will include the treatments, tank numbers and other important identifier details.
The purpose of this DMP will be to ensure that the data created in this experiment is stored properly and shared with the public as needed.
description.label: AQUASERV | all categories | generic template

descriptionTemplate.label: AQUASERV | all categories | generic template
descriptionTemplate.description: A template of Horizon Europe adopted for AQUASERV

fieldSet.title: Will you re-use any existing data and what will you re-use it for? State the reasons if re-use of any existing data has been considered but discarded.
What types and formats of data will the project generate or re-use?
What is the purpose of the data generation or re-use and its relation to the objectives of the project?
What is the expected size of the data that you intend to generate or re-use?
What is the origin/provenance of the data, either generated or re-used?
To whom might your data be useful ('data utility'), outside your project?
Will data be identified by a persistent identifier?

Will rich metadata be provided to allow discovery? What metadata will be created? What disciplinary or general standards will be followed? In case metadata standards do not exist in your discipline, please outline what type of metadata will be created and how.
Will search keywords be provided in the metadata to optimize the possibility for discovery and then potential re-use?
Will metadata be offered in such a way that it can be harvested and indexed?

section.title: Making data findable, including provisions for metadata

fieldSet.title: Will the data be deposited in a trusted repository?
Have you explored appropriate arrangements with the identified repository where your data will be deposited?
Does the repository ensure that the data is assigned an identifier? Will the repository resolve the identifier to a digital object?

Will all data be made openly available? If certain datasets cannot be shared (or need to be shared under restricted access conditions), explain why, clearly separating legal and contractual reasons from intentional restrictions. Note that in multi-beneficiary projects it is also possible for specific beneficiaries to keep their data closed if opening their data goes against their legitimate interests or other constraints as per the Grant Agreement.

If an embargo is applied to give time to publish or seek protection of the intellectual property (e.g. patents), specify why and how long this will apply, bearing in mind that research data should be made available as soon as possible.
Will the data be accessible through a free and standardized access protocol?
If there are restrictions on use, how will access be provided to the data, both during and after the end of the project?
How will the identity of the person accessing the data be ascertained?
Is there a need for a data access committee (e.g. to evaluate/approve access requests to personal/sensitive data)?

Will metadata be made openly available and licenced under a public domain dedication CC0, as per the Grant Agreement? If not, please clarify why. Will metadata contain information to enable the user to access the data?
How long will the data remain available and findable? Will metadata be guaranteed to remain available after data is no longer available?
Will documentation or reference about any software be needed to access or read the data be included? Will it be possible to include the relevant software (e.g. in open source code)?

What data and metadata vocabularies, standards, formats or methodologies will you follow to make your data interoperable to allow data exchange and re-use within and across disciplines? Will you follow community-endorsed interoperability best practices? Which ones?

In case it is unavoidable that you use uncommon or generate project specific ontologies or vocabularies, will you provide mappings to more commonly used ontologies? Will you openly publish the generated ontologies or vocabularies to allow reusing, refining or extending them?
Will your data include qualified references to other data (e.g. other data from your project, or datasets from previous research)?

How will you provide documentation needed to validate data analysis and facilitate data re-use (e.g. readme files with information on methodology, codebooks, data cleaning, analyses, variable definitions, units of measurement, etc.)?

Will your data be made freely available in the public domain to permit the widest re-use possible? Will your data be licensed using standard reuse licenses, in line with the obligations set out in the Grant Agreement?
Will the data produced in the project be useable by third parties, in particular after the end of the project?
Will the provenance of the data be thoroughly documented using the appropriate standards?
Describe all relevant data quality assurance processes.
What provisions are or will be in place for data security (including data recovery as well as secure storage/archiving and transfer of sensitive data)?
Will the data be safely stored in trusted repositories for long term preservation and curation?
Will other research outputs be identified by a persistent identifier?

Will rich metadata be provided to allow discovery? What metadata will be created? What disciplinary or general standards will be followed? In case metadata standards do not exist in your discipline, please outline what type of metadata will be created and how.
Will search keywords be provided in the metadata to optimize the possibility for discovery and then potential re-use?
Will metadata be offered in such a way that it can be harvested and indexed?

section.description:
In addition to the management of data, beneficiaries should also consider and plan for the management of other research outputs that may be generated or re-used throughout their projects. Such outputs can be either digital (e.g. software, workflows, protocols, models, etc.) or physical (e.g. new materials, antibodies, reagents, samples, etc.).

Beneficiaries should consider which of the questions pertaining to FAIR data above, can apply to the management of other research outputs, and should strive to provide sufficient detail on how their research outputs will be managed and shared, or made available for re-use, in line with the FAIR principles.
section.title: Making other research outputs findable, including provisions for metadata

fieldSet.title: Will the other research outputs be deposited in a trusted repository?
Have you explored appropriate arrangements with the identified repository where your other research outputs will be deposited?
Does the repository ensure that the other research outputs is assigned an identifier? Will the repository resolve the identifier to a digital object?

Will all other research outputs be made openly available? If certain research outputs cannot be shared (or need to be shared under restricted access conditions), explain why, clearly separating legal and contractual reasons from intentional restrictions. Note that in multi-beneficiary projects it is also possible for specific beneficiaries to keep their research outputs closed if opening their research outputs goes against their legitimate interests or other constraints as per the Grant Agreement.

If an embargo is applied to give time to publish or seek protection of the intellectual property (e.g. patents), specify why and how long this will apply, bearing in mind that research outputs should be made available as soon as possible.
Will the other research outputs be accessible through a free and standardized access protocol?

labelData.label: Please specify other research outputs access process

fieldSet.title: If there are restrictions on use, how will access be provided to the other research outputs, both during and after the end of the project?
How will the identity of the person accessing the other research outputs be ascertained?
Is there a need for an other research output access committee (e.g. to evaluate/approve access requests to personal/sensitive other research output)?

Will metadata be made openly available and licenced under a public domain dedication CC0, as per the Grant Agreement? If not, please clarify why. Will metadata contain information to enable the user to access the other research outputs?
How long will the other research outputs remain available and findable? Will metadata be guaranteed to remain available after other research outputs is no longer available?
Will documentation or reference about any software be needed to access or read the other research outputs be included? Will it be possible to include the relevant software (e.g. in open source code)?

What other research outputs and metadata vocabularies, standards, formats or methodologies will you follow to make your other research outputs interoperable to allow other research outputs exchange and re-use within and across disciplines? Will you follow community-endorsed interoperability best practices? Which ones?

In case it is unavoidable that you use uncommon or generate project specific ontologies or vocabularies, will you provide mappings to more commonly used ontologies? Will you openly publish the generated ontologies or vocabularies to allow reusing, refining or extending them?
Will your other research outputs include qualified references to other research outputs (e.g. other research outputs from your project, or other research outputs from previous research)?

How will you provide documentation needed to validate other research outputs analysis and facilitate other research outputs re-use (e.g. readme files with information on methodology, codebooks, research outputs cleaning, analyses, variable definitions, units of measurement, etc.)?

Will your other research outputs be made freely available in the public domain to permit the widest re-use possible? Will your other research outputs be licensed using standard reuse licenses, in line with the obligations set out in the Grant Agreement?
Will the other research outputs produced in the project be useable by third parties, in particular after the end of the project?
Will the provenance of the other research outputs be thoroughly documented using the appropriate standards?
Describe all relevant other research outputs quality assurance processes.

Further to the FAIR principles, DMPs should also address research outputs other than data, and should carefully consider aspects related to the allocation of resources, research outputs security and ethical aspects.
What will the costs be for making data or other research outputs FAIR in your project (e.g. direct and indirect costs related to storage, archiving, re-use, security, etc.) ?
How will these be covered?
fieldSet.description: Note that costs related to research data/output management are eligible as part of the Horizon Europe grant (if compliant with the Grant Agreement conditions).

fieldSet.title: Who will be responsible for data management in your project?
How will long term preservation be ensured? Discuss the necessary resources to accomplish this (costs and potential value, who decides and how, what data will be kept and for how long)?

Are there, or could there be, any ethics or legal issues that can have an impact on data sharing? These can also be discussed in the context of the ethics review. If relevant, include references to ethics deliverables and ethics chapter in the Description of the Action (DoA).
Will informed consent for data sharing and long term preservation be included in questionnaires dealing with personal data?
Do you, or will you, make use of other national/funder/sectorial/departmental procedures for data management? If yes, which ones (please list and briefly describe them)?

field.textValue: I will provide a ReadMe file that will include descriptions of all the variables in the other research outputs. In addition, project specific vocabulary will be defined in the ReadMe file to allow for other users to reuse, refine or extend their definitions.
It will be put into the DataDryad repository.
All of the other research outputs can be shared, but will be embargoed for a time period to allow for publication of a corresponding journal article.
There are no restrictions on use.
There will not be questionnaires dealing with personal data.

The data generated will help us determine if the fish are responding to the experimental conditions which are mimicking future ocean conditions under climate change. The data will be analyzed and response variables from each treatment compared to determine differences in fish response from control, ambient waters to those expected under future climate scenarios.
It will be published in the DataDryad repository.
Yes. This will mean mainly creating additional columns in datasheets when transformations are applied rather than replacing the original data in the sheet.

It will be associated with a DOI when published to the DataDryad repository. Each specific datapoint or sample will be given an identifier that will be a combination of the specimen number, date measured, replicate number, and treatment information.
NA

The main quality assurance process for other research outputs will be the testing of the different written protocols by someone who is unfamiliar with the process to ensure that they are thorough enough. This will allow for changes before the experiment begins.
Additionally, the labels on samples will be checked by two people to ensure that the right specimen is going into the correct sample container.
There will not be restrictions on use.

I will provide a ReadMe file that will include descriptions of all the variables in each spreadsheet including units, allowing users to connect the concepts to more commonly used vocabularies. In addition, project specific vocabulary will be defined in the ReadMe file to allow for other users to reuse, refine or extend their definitions.

Data might be useful for future meta analyses looking at the effects of climate change on marine organisms. The data could also be useful in population modeling used by fisheries departments that is done to help create catch limits.

The main indirect cost will be the time required to put data in appropriate formatting for storage and archiving. These will be a part of the grant that pays Emma Siegfried - NSF-ORCC #2307813. 
There will also be a small direct cost in the sense of storing the data on DataDryad which will likely cost around $150 USD.
Information about the software needed to read or access the data will be included in the publication if necessary.
The expected size will be on the order of MB of data.
Indirect cost

An embargo will be applied for approximately 1 year to give time for data analysis and publication. The meta data including experimental treatments will be made available soon after the experiment is completed, but the rest of the experimental response data will be held until a publication has been accepted.
The repository assigns a DOI.

When measuring fish, two different individuals will measure the fish. If the measurements are more than 1.0 mm different, a third individual will measure the fish. The average of the lengths will then be used as the measurement of the fish length.
In addition, measurements of seawater chemistry will be done in triplicate to ensure accuracy of the measurements. In addition, these samples will be compared to a certified reference material and corrected for error as needed.
Data entry will be completed with two people. One reading out values and a second typing to help ensure that data is entered correctly.
Yes. This will mean mainly creating additional columns in datasheets when transformations are applied rather than replacing the original data in the sheet.
The data will be generated during the experiment.

Other research outputs that might be in the process would be protocols and samples. Each specific datapoint or sample will be given an identifier that will be a combination of the specimen number, date measured, replicate number, and treatment information.

All of the information that is collected will be placed in a spreadsheet. At the beginning of the experiment I will note the address where the laboratory work will happen. On a daily basis, the names of the people observing the embryos and larvae as well as noting the actual date. I will also create a list of all the different instruments that are used, specifically what type of equipment is used to dose the different tanks with CO2 levels and what instruments are used to measure the pH, salinity, etc. of the water. Finally, I will create documentation for each protocol that I use during this experiment as well as throughout the analysis of the samples and data. This will all be included as supplementary material when I publish my data.

All of the information that is collected will be placed in a spreadsheet. At the beginning of the experiment I will note the address where the laboratory work will happen. On a daily basis, the names of the people observing the embryos and larvae will be noted as well as the actual date. I will also create a list of all the different instruments that are used, specifically what type of equipment is used to dose the different tanks with CO2 levels and what instruments are used to measure the pH, salinity, etc. of the water. Finally, I will create documentation for each protocol that I use during this experiment as well as throughout the analysis of the samples and data. This will all be included as supplementary material when I publish my data.
The data will remain available and findable indefinitely after publishing.
The data will be uploaded into the DataDryad repository.
NA
I will be collecting measurements from this experiment in a spreadsheet (.csv and .xlsx) and potentially images of fish as well (.jpg and .png).

I will provide a ReadMe file that will include descriptions of all the variables in each spreadsheet including units. In addition, project specific vocabulary will be defined in the ReadMe file to allow for other users to reuse, refine or extend their definitions.

Data Dryad is a data repository where you can upload datafiles. Once the experiment is completed and all of the data cleaned, it will be uploaded onto this website. There is a fee for uploading to this website, but it is well maintained and user friendly so that the data can be easily found and used by other scientists. Data will be stored here indefinitely.
Data Dryad
Data will be stored on OneDrive - a cloud based storage system. This data will be in this storage location for approximately 3 years after the data is created.
OneDrive
All of the datasets can be shared, but will be embargoed for a time period to allow for publication of a corresponding journal article.
Information about the software needed to read or access the data will be included in the publication if necessary.

I will provide a ReadMe file that will include descriptions of all the variables in each spreadsheet including units, allowing users to connect the concepts to more commonly used vocabularies. In addition, project specific vocabulary will be defined in the ReadMe file to allow for other users to reuse, refine or extend their definitions.
The data will remain available and findable indefinitely after publishing.

The EU has a Guide to best practices for ocean acidification research and data reporting. I will follow these best practices to the best of my abilities. Otherwise, I will consult with AQUASERV to ensure that my data is as interoperable as possible.

An embargo will be applied for approximately 1 year to give time for data analysis and publication. The meta data including experimental treatments will be made available soon after the experiment is completed, but the rest of the experimental response data will be held until a publication has been accepted.

The EU has a Guide to best practices for ocean acidification research and data reporting. I will follow these best practices to the best of my abilities. Otherwise, I will consult with AQUASERV to ensure that my data is as interoperable as possible.
There will not be any sensitive data included in this experiment.
Examples of keywords that will be provided in the metadata include: ocean acidification, larval fish, Ammodytes, climate change, and early life history.
Examples of keywords that will be provided in the metadata include: ocean acidification, larval fish, Ammodytes, climate change, and early life history.
The data will be assigned a DOI.

description.description:
This DMP is part of a project that will be happening at the Institute of Marine Research (IMR) that is focused on the effect of ocean acidification on Ammodytes marinus embryos. The data that will be collected are daily hatch frequencies, hatching success, time to hatch, and body size metrics at hatch. This will be in addition to metadata which will include the treatments, tank numbers and other important identifier details.
The purpose of this DMP will be to ensure that the data created in this experiment is stored properly and shared with the public as needed.
description.label: AQUASERV template

descriptionTemplate.label: AQUASERV template
descriptionTemplate.description: A template of Horizon Europe adopted and simplified for AQUASERV

fieldSet.title: Will you re-use any existing data and what will you re-use it for? State the reasons if re-use of any existing data has been considered but discarded.
fieldSet.description:
example: Re-use existing data

I will look for data (occurrences of a range of species) from EMODnet to complement my study of biodiversity of the Baltic Sea in 2025

fieldSet.title: What types and formats of data will the project generate or re-use?
fieldSet.description:
example: 
Type of data
    I will be collecting images (jpg or png, probably), sound files (probably mp4) and videos (mp4)
    I will be collecting DNA sequences (FASTQ) and creating bioinformatics outputs (FASTA, TSV, TXT)
    I will be collecting measurements from my aquaculture experiment in a spreadsheet
    I will collect images (jpg or png, probably) and spreadsheets (CSV or excel) from a FlowCam

fieldSet.title: What is the expected size of the data that you intend to generate or re-use?
What is the origin/provenance of the data, either generated or re-used?
In which database, catalogue, or portal will you publish your raw and/or processed data
fieldSet.description:
example: 
Where publish

    I will publish my data on OBIS
    I will publish my sequences on ENA
    I will publish my spreadsheets on zenodo
    I will publish my data on my university repository (University of Somewhere, https://university.of.somewhere.eu/data) because that is mandatory, but I will also publish my data on OBIS

fieldSet.title: How will you collect the data discovery and description metadata to describe the following aspects of your data: WHO (did the work) WHEN (was the work, sampling done) WHAT (parameters, samples, data was collected) WHAT (instruments, software, platforms was used) WHERE (was the work, the sampling done) HOW (was the sampling, processing, experiment, software analysis done)
fieldSet.description:
Metadata are contextual information that you will provide when you publish your data, to make those data findable, re-usable and reproducible

EXAMPLE:
I will collect this information in a special spreadsheet: the names and orcids of all the people, the location of the sample collection, the address of the lab, the dates of sample collection and processing. I will ask the TA service provider for the names of all the instruments and devices I will use and for the protocol documents ( as a PDF or as a web address) that I will use. I will also create a document for each of my own protocols for my own processing and experimental work. I will then add all this information when I publish my data

section.title: Making data findable, including provisions for metadata

fieldSet.title: Is the database, catalogue or portal where you will publish your data Open Access ?
fieldSet.description:
by Open Access we mean that anybody should be able to find, view and download from that database/catalogue/portal

by PUBLISH we mean upload it to a public database/portal/catalogue (i.e. not peer-review) - not necessary to a scientific journal

fieldSet.title: Does the repository ensure that the data is assigned an identifier? Will the repository resolve the identifier to a digital object?
What licence will you publish your data under ? https://creativecommons.org/share-your-work/cclicenses/

If an embargo is applied to give time to publish or seek protection of the intellectual property (e.g. patents), specify why and how long this will apply, bearing in mind that research data should be made available as soon as possible.
If there are restrictions on use (e.g. IP issues, sensitive data), how will access be provided to the data, both during and after the end of the project?
fieldSet.description:
example answer: 

 I am collecting data about sensitive species, and so will keep some information (specific locations for example) private
WHAT was our decision about embargo for scientific publication? We should give an example of an answer that is about restrictions for embargo periods. Something like: I will make a metadata record for my data in Zendo, but I will embargo the data until my scientific publication has been accepted, and I will then make the data fully accessible.
Some of my data will be extremely large. I would like advise on how to publish these data and how to make them accessible for the long-term  (OF COURSE allowing answers like this means we have to read the DMPs and get in touch with the person to help them)

fieldSet.title: Unpublishable data - is there any dataset you do not want to or can't publish (i.e. make public) and why? (Null data should be published - i.e. data that is not useful to your science and hence will not be published in scientific journal should nevertheless be made Open Access)
fieldSet.description: for example - extremely large dataset, sensitive data, potential patent pending data

fieldSet.title: Will the metadata be Open Access ?
fieldSet.description:
by Open Access we mean that anybody should be able to read and download a copy of those metadata

example answer:
I will create a project-metadata-spreadsheet to collect all the metadata to describe my dataset for when I publish that data. In this I will record the names and ORCIDs of the people involved, the lat, long and marine region of any locations, dates in YYYY-MM-DD format for all activities. I will also include the name and link (URL) of all protocols that I will use (whether they are my own or from the TA station), and of all software I will use. I will ask the station for the names and types of all the instruments and devices I will use and will also write that into the spreadsheet. I will include this spreadsheet as part of my dataset when I publish it.

fieldSet.title: Are your data formats open or closed?
fieldSet.description:
A closed format needs proprietary software/application to be read (i.e. Excel xlsx vs .csv file) or it is an instrument specific format  

example answer:
My data will be in open formats - jpg, png, mp4, FASTQ
The instruments I will use at the TA station produce data in proprietary formats: I will discuss with the TA station if and how I can convert these to open formats
I will convert my excel files to CSV before I publish them, so they are in an open format

fieldSet.title: Will you use controlled vocabularies in your data?
fieldSet.description:
controlled vocabularies are terms that are agreed by the scientific communities and captured in the following examples:
https://genomicsstandardsconsortium.github.io/mixs/
https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/envo
https://www.marinespecies.org/
https://www.marineregions.org/
https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/search_nvs/
https://agrovoc.fao.org/skosmosAsfa/asfa/en/

example answer:

I will use terms from ENVO to describe the habitats relevant to my experiment. I will use NVS to describe measured parameters and their units.
I will use MiXS when uploading my data to ENA, as this is mandatory
I do not have much experience with using vocabularies, but I will make sure that I use those mentioned in the example and will ask AQUASERV for advise.

fieldSet.title: Will all software settings be Open Access?
fieldSet.description: example: I will provided my software settings in protocols.io, or in a file in GitHub, or in a text file in my dataset

fieldSet.title: Will you provide the names and web address (URL) of all the software you used in your analysis? Is any of that software closed access (e.g. commercial)?
fieldSet.description: example: your own software at GitHub repository

fieldSet.title: Will your experimental protocols be Open Access?
fieldSet.description: you can use protocols.io for example (there is AQUASERV community there)

fieldSet.title: Please now send an email to aquaservhelpdesk@ualg.pt before submitting this DMP on zenod. We will review this DMP and provide a feedback

section.description: any other information you would like to provide
section.title: Other issues

field.textValue: The datasets will be provided in open formats - .csv and .png.
I will publish my data on the Dryad database.

I will use terms from the WoRMS as a way to identify species used in this experiment and potentially the Marine regions. I also will make sure that talk to AQUASERV to ensure that the appropriate vocabularies are used.
Yes
There will be an embargo to give time to publish. This should be hopefully be within a year, and the data will be published as soon as the journal article is accepted.

All of the information that is collected will be placed in a spreadsheet. At the beginning of the experiment I will note the address where the laboratory work will happen. On a daily basis, the names of the people observing the embryos and larvae as well as noting the actual date will be recorded. I will also create a list of all the different instruments that are used, specifically what type of equipment is used to dose the different tanks with CO2 levels and what instruments are used to measure the pH, salinity, etc. of the water. Metadata will also include all of the different treatment levels that individual fish will be exposed to. These will be measured throughout the experiment and recorded in a spreadsheet. Finally, I will create documentation for each protocol that I use during this experiment as well as throughout the analysis of the samples and data. This will all be included as supplementary material when I publish my data.
Citations will be included in the published journal article for the software used for analysis.
I will publish under a CC-BY licence.
The repository assigns the datasets a DOI.

I am going to embargo my data on response variables specifically until my scientific publication has been accepted. The values describing the experimental treatments and other metadata can be published ahead of time, but responses of organisms will not be published until the data is accepted.
I don't believe there are any.
Yes. I will include a text file in my data set for any software settings as needed.
The data will be generated during the experiment.

The metadata will be open access and published along with the other data. This will include the location where the experiment is done, the names and potential ORCIDs of anyone who is involved. I will also include dates for each activity and treatment levels.
Finally, I will ensure that the instrument names and models are also in a text sheet that will be included in my published data.
I will be collecting measurements from this experiment in a spreadsheet (.csv and .xlsx) and potentially images of fish as well (.jpg and .png).
This will likely be on the order of MB of data.
Protocols will not be open access, but they will be part of the journal article published from this dataset.
